title: trifolium longipes subsp. longipes
intro: Trifolium longipes subsp. longipes, also known as long-styled Indian clover, is a subspecies of perennial herb belonging to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and pink or white flowers. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Trifolium longipes subsp. longipes include its potential to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic. Traditionally, the plant can be prepared in herbal teas or infusions, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed for various health purposes.
